We can’t wait to see you there – Happy TechEd! 😊

entry.title: Devtoberfest 2024 - Tooling (SAP Build) Sessions
entry.content:
Today is the first day of Devoberfest!!

Make sure you partake of the:

  • Contest -- grand prize is a trip to an SAP TechEd event with a lot of extras; and even though it's several years' old I still love the gameboard
  • Petoberfest – post a picture of your pet 🐢
  • Sessions – find all the sessions in the event calendar 
  • 👉 The annual SAP Crossword Puzzle – if you have the skill, you can earn 1800 points!

Devtoberfest 2024 sessions

Here's a quick rundown of the SAP Build "Tooling Tuesday" sessions, which touch on the following topics:

  • SAP Build Apps
  • SAP Build Process Automation
  • SAP Build Work Zone
  • SAP Build Code
  • MDK
  • S/4HANA + extensions
  • Integration Suite, including event mesh
  • CAP
  • AI Core
  • GenAI + Joule
  • SAP Cloud Transport Management

That's just one day of Devtoberfest!! In each session, you'll have a chance to ask questions directly to the presenter, who will answer them live or in the chat.

September 24

  • 🟣 Transform Data with Scripting in SAP Build Process Automation

  • 🟣 Develop a Full-Stack CAP Application with SAP Build Code

  • 🟣 Develop with SAP Build Apps Like a Pro

  • 🟣 Real-World S/4HANA Invoice Approval Process with SAP Build Process Automation

October 1

  • 🟣 SAP Build Apps 2049: Soon with UI5 and GenAI?

  • 🟣 Enable Work Zone to Consume Business Solutions from Other BTP Subaccounts (content federation)

  • 🟣 Cross-Platform Mobile App Development with SAP Build Code and the MDK

  • 🟣 Putting It All Together: S/4HANA Retail Pricing App Using SAP Build, AI, Event Mesh and More

October 15

  • 🟣 S/4HANA Extensions: Start Fast with the New Extensibility Wizard

  • 🟣 Guided Experiences with SAP Build Process Automation and Work Zone

  • 🟣 Develop Even Faster (!) with SAP Build Apps Templates

  • 🟣 Boost Development with GenAI in SAP Build Process Automation

October 22

  • 🟣 Chatbot Development Made Easy with SAP AI Core and Build Apps

  • 🟣 Set Up Rules for Who Can Create and Transport SAP Build Projects

  • 🟣 Leveraging Decisions and Business Rules in SAP Build Process Automation

  • 🟣 Extend Joule with Your Own Skills

Learn a lot about SAP development tools, have a lot of fun – and maybe we'll see you at an SAP TechEd event next year 😸

entry.title: Enabling Email Notifications in SAP Build Work Zone via Microsoft 365 Exchange Online (OAuth-Based)
entry.content:
Email notifications are an essential component for organizations using SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Task Center and other products delivered by SAP to keep users informed about updates, events, and tasks, see a diagram below as a reference visualizing SAP Task Center use case about sending  email notifications.

Microsoft announced the deprecation of Basic Authentication for Exchange Online (as outlined here). It means users of Microsoft 365 must now switch to OAuth-based authentication for sending email notifications. This change impacts users of SAP Build Work Zone and other SAP cloud products like SAP Cloud ALM and SAP Task Center.Thus, to ensure email notifications continue functioning, users of Microsoft 365, must configure SAP BTP subaccount destination for email notifications in SAP Build Work Zone using OAuth Client Credentials Flow (applicable to Microsoft 365 Exchange Online).

Together with my team-mate Angel Tonchev, we have noticed that SAP customers experience challenges to setup Microsoft 365 Exchange Online to OAuth-based authentication and then use these properties to setup the correctly SMTP Mail Destination in SAP BTP. This is why, we joint forces to prepate this guide. I hope you will find it useful! 

Prerequisites

Before configuring the Internet mail destination with OAuth Client Credentials Flow in your SAP BTP subaccount, ensure that notifications for SAP Build Work Zone have already been enabled. Follow the instructions outlined in the official SAP documentation here to set up notifications from your cloud solutions.

Configuring OAuth-Based Authentication for Microsoft 365

To generate the needed OAuth-Based Authentication properties, these configurations are to be done in your Microsoft 365 account:

  1. Open “office.com”. Click on the dots in the upper left corner and choose “Admin”
  2. On the left panel choose “Show all”

     

    3. After that, choose “All admin centers” and from there choose “Microsoft Entra"

3.1. In the Microsoft Entra admin center, navigate to Identity -> Applications -> App registration and choose “New registration”

4. Enter a name and click Register

5. On next window, choose “Certificates and secrets” -> “New client secret”

Note down the Value as it is a one-time view:

6. Next, on the left-side menu  choose: “API permissions” and then "Add a permission"

7. From there, choose “APIs my organization uses” -> “Office 365 Exchange Online”

8. Then, choose “Application permissions”, search for “smtp” and select “SMTP.SendAsApp”. Choose “Add permissions”

9. On next window choose “Grant admin consent for MSFT”

10. App registration - Overview page:  note down Application (client) ID and Directory (tenant) ID

Register a Microsoft Entra app and create a service principal

Once that’s done, you need to create a dedicated technical user email for sending alerts. You can decide which user is going to be used for sending emails or you can decide to create a new technical one for that.

1. From Microsoft Admin Center, navigate to: Users -> Add a user and follow the procedure as per the screenshot below.

Then, from Microsoft Admin Center, navigate to Users-> Active Users, and select the created / newly added user:
- click “Mail” under the user -> select “Manage email apps”
- make sure that “Authenticated SMTP” is checked.

Register the app service principle in Exchange Online with PowerShell or to the Azure environment (in case you have access). To do so , please check the documentation here: Register a Microsoft Entra app and create a service principal

Note: you can also consult the official documentation for PowerShell

In PowerShell:
1. Run line  “Install-Module -Name ExchangeOnlineManagement -allowprerelease” (on error remove “– allowprerelease”)
2. Run line  “Import-module ExchangeOnlineManagement”
3. Run line “Connect-ExchangeOnline -Organization <Directory (tenant) ID>”
NOTE: “Directory (tenant) ID” which we noted down in “Procedure” step #10
4. Run line “New-ServicePrincipal -AppId < Application (client) ID > -ObjectId <Object ID>”

NOTE: “Application (client) ID” which we noted down in “Procedure” step #10
 The Object ID is different than the one that we used in the one visible on APP creation. To get the right onе, go the “Microsoft Entra admin center”->“Identity“->“Applications“->“Enterprise applications“->“All Applications“. Select your app->from “Overview”, you can copy the Object ID

5.Run line “ Add-MailboxPermission -Identity "username@domain.com" -User  <Object ID> -AccessRights FullAccess”
NOTE: For “Identity” use the user created specially created in the “Create dedicated User” section

That’s an example on how the outcome should look like:

Configure a Destination service in your SAP BTP subaccount

Now as we are ready with all the needed configurations from Microsoft365 side, we proceed with applying the needed properties in your Destination service in your SAP BTP subaccount. To do so, please follow the steps listed underneath:

1. Access your SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) cockpit.

2. Go to the Destinations section under your subaccount and click “Create Destination”

3. Create a new destination for the SMTP Mail server with the properties described in this procedure, see point (b) Internet mail destination with OAuth Client Credentials Flow :

Example destination:

    • Name: SAP_Business_Notifications_Mail
    • Type: Mail
    • Proxy Type: Internet
    • Authentication: OAuth2ClientCredentials
    • Client ID: <Application (client) ID ” see step #10 “App registration - Overview page”
    • Client Secret: <Secret ID>, see step #5: “Certificates and secrets” -> “New client secret”
    • Token Service URL Type: Dedicated
    • Token Service URL: https://login.microsoftonline.com/<Tenant ID>/oauth2/v2.0/token
      Note: for <Tenant ID> use the “Directory (tenant) ID” from step #10 “App registration- Overview page”

 Additional Properties:

    • mail.smtp.host: smtp.office365.com
    • mail.smtp.port: 587
    • mail.username: <technical_user_email_address>
    • mail.smtp.from: <technical_user_email_address>
    • scope: https://outlook.office365.com/.default
    • [optional] mail.smtp.timeout: 5000

Testing Your Configuration

Once the configuration is complete, send a test email notification from SAP Build Work Zone to ensure everything is working as expected, see a test email I was able to receive shared below:.

Recap

With the deprecation of Basic Authentication in Microsoft 365 Exchange Online, it’s crucial for SAP Build Work Zone users to switch to OAuth-based authentication for SMTP Mail destinations. This guide has provided step-by-step instructions for configuring Microsoft 365 OAuth Client Credentials Flow, ensuring seamless email notifications for SAP cloud products.

This configuration not only applies to SAP Build Work Zone but can also be used for other SAP products like SAP Cloud ALM and SAP Task Center, enabling secure and reliable email notifications.

We hope this guide has been helpful. If you have any questions or suggestions, we’re open to your feedback! Feel free to share your thoughts in the comments.

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Build – Q3 2024 Edition
entry.content:
Greetings, Builders! We’re pleased to bring you the Q3 Edition of our What’s New in SAP Build series. In this edition, we’re covering product-specific updates for:

  • SAP Build Apps
  • SAP Build Process Automation
  • SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Start, SAP Task Center
  • SAP Build Code

SAP Build Apps

Release management and project lifecycle management: It’s now possible to release and transport projects across different tenants using SAP Cloud Transport Management System. You can transport project versions between subaccounts while isolating development, testing, and productive deployment of SAP Build Apps applications to their own landscapes. To get started, see this blog post.

Performant table list with large dataset from S/4HANA: Managing and displaying large amounts of data in an application can be challenging and cause performance issues. Check out this example of creating a performant list page for an S/4HANA Cloud endpoint with 65k+ records - see the blog post.

Copying pages within one project: This feature enables users to copy and duplicate a page within a project in SAP Build Apps – a simple, yet powerful, add-on to further speed up development.

New Build Service: We recently launched a new and improved experience for deploying application packages. The build service includes managing image assets, iOS usage strings, mobile service enablement, package creation, and configuration management. The updated UI provides a much smoother experience for developers at the deployment phase, ensuring that every requirement is met and that your apps are ready to publish. Try it out today!

  • Also check out Ludo Noens’ series on Consuming SAP with SAP Build Apps – Creating a CRUD Application
  • Learn more about what’s coming up on the SAP Build Apps Roadmap.

SAP Build Process Automation

Script Task Editor: It's now possible to write JavaScript code in the new Script Editor, allowing the execution of complex data type transformations and additional operations on the process context. Previously this was limited to automation - now it includes process inputs and custom variables, facilitating advanced data manipulation and transformation. See more in the blog post.

Scheduled trigger: Now you can schedule process automations based on user-defined time intervals and dates at runtime. This allows you to enable system-centric and time-based process automation use cases, such as working and nonworking days in creating your automation scenarios to avoid resource mismatches.

Additional Outcomes in Forms and UI5 Support for Tasks: This enables citizen developers to configure additional outcome actions in the process to let approvers request rework in case of missing or wrong information in approval forms. You can also start filling out multiple forms efficiently through a guided experience.

Advanced Event Project Creation with Manual Specification Upload: This allows users to upload business event specifications for the creation of an event project in the lobby. The new feature adds support for business events across lines of business (LOBs) and custom events, including third-party events that are supported at runtime using SAP Event Mesh.

Process Visibility enhancements: These include actionable insights, instance tracking for requestors and additional monitoring capabilities via CALM. Now you can turn insights into meaningful outcomes and enable the triggering of escalation flows based on visibility insights, to directly react and resolve critical situations. This enables auditable and governed escalation resolution, instead of having disconnected communication through different channels.

More on SAP Build Process Automation:

  • See the What's New Viewer for more updates, and improvements.
  • See SAP Road Map Explorer for more upcoming features and focus areas.

SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Start, and SAP Task Center

SAP Build Work Zone, both editions

HTML5 Business Solution as a Content Provider: With the latest update in SAP Build Work Zone, you can now expose BTP-based applications as Work Zone content, like roles, spaces, and pages, in the 'Common Data Model' (CDM) format. As a result, you can easily configure and enable access to BTP applications from a Work Zone instance, even across subaccounts. See more in the Help Portal and this blog post.

SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition

Define Sort Order of pages and spaces: Administrators can now configure how spaces and pages are sorted on the menu bar of SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition. This feature is only applicable if you're working in the new experience view mode for spaces and pages. See more in the Help Portal.

Import and Export of Pages and Spaces: Content manager tools will be enhanced to enable administrators to export and import custom (locally created) spaces and pages between landscapes.

SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition

Integrated guided experience across SAP Build Work Zone and Process Automation*: Guided processes are now available as an artifact in SAP Build Process Automation. In addition to the previously available “no template” wizards, it is now possible to create guided, process-based wizards in SAP Build Work Zone.

*Note: This will become GA in October 2024. 

SAP Start

Directly navigate to product home pages of integrated cloud solutions: The SAP Start site offers a new product section to easily see and navigate to the product home pages of the integrated solutions. This enables transparency of available cloud solutions for the current user, and quick and easy navigation to launch the product home page of the desired solution.

SAP Task Center

ITCE partner page: SAP Task Center now allows connections with third-party task providers. The first solution to leverage this capability is TaskConnect, developed by ITCE in collaboration with SAP architects and technical consultants. The application will soon be available on the SAP Store as well, offering a comprehensive configuration guide, installation manuals and BPMN diagrams. For more information about TaskConnect, see here.

More on SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Start and SAP Task Center:

  • Check out the roadmaps for SAP Build Work Zone standard & advanced edition and SAP Start / SAP Task Center
  • What's New Viewers: Standard edition / Advanced edition / SAP Start / SAP Task Center 

SAP Build Code

New AI Features: In SAP Build Code, you can now use the Joule copilot, our generative AI assistant, to write code by providing natural language prompts. For a detailed explanation of Joule, please read this article. In the last months we added many new features to this capability including the following highlights:

  • Support SAP S/4HANA Cloud Service Integration for Data Model and App Logic: It is now possible to use Joule to generate code for your integrated SAP S/4HANA Cloud services.
  • Major UX Update of Joule in SAP Build Code: With the latest UX update of Joule within SAP Build Code we enhanced the usability, streamlined workflows, and created a more intuitive interface, boosting productivity and satisfaction.
  • Generative AI support for productivity tools: We now allow you to use Joule directly from the storyboard.
  • Use Joule to enhance your already existing data models and services: It is now possible to modify already existing data model and service entities using a prompt.

Why SAP Build Code: Check out this blog post by Product Manager Rupa Maity describing why to move to SAP Build Code.

Stay updated on the latest Build Code innovations: Do you want to stay informed about the latest product updates for SAP Build Code? Follow our tag on SAP Community and subscribe to our SAP Build Code Newsletter. 

  • See the latest update product updates from September 2024 for SAP Build Code.
  • Learn more on the SAP Build Code Community.

--- 

💡Please note this blog post provides a highlight of the latest features and enhancements. 

  • For comprehensive list of all updates, please refer to our Help Portal What’s New in SAP Build.
  • Join the SAP Builders group for product resources and community questions.
  • Devtoberfest is here! 🥨 Don't miss our SAP Build-themed sessions and more.
  • ...and that means SAP TechEd is right around the corner! Explore SAP Build at TechEd 2024.

Which feature are you most excited to test out? Let us know in the comments!

entry.title: SAP Build sessions for week 2 of Devtoberfest
entry.content:
👉𝗔𝗿𝗲 𝘆𝗼𝘂 into UI5 and GenAI (AI) and want to see a sneak peek how SAP Build Apps might support it?


👉𝗔𝗿𝗲 𝘆𝗼𝘂 a full-stack developer working with CAP or UI5 and want to know the proper way to prepare your app to be consumed across BTP tenants?


👉𝗔𝗿𝗲 𝘆𝗼𝘂 an experienced backend or frontend developer, or citizen developer, who wants to quickly build sophisticated mobile apps for both iOS and Android, using SAP Build Code and without needing to know Swift or Android/Kotlin tools?


👉𝗔𝗿𝗲 𝘆𝗼𝘂 an SAP developer or citizen developer who wants to learn how to put all the BTP services together – Integration Suite, SAP Build, AI – to create real-world sophisticated S/4HANA extensions?


Enjoy today's 𝗧𝗼𝗼𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗧𝘂𝗲𝘀𝗱𝗮𝘆 (𝗦𝗔𝗣 𝗕𝘂𝗶𝗹𝗱) sessions, and continue your Devtoberfest experience!

entry.title: 🚀Fire of Innovations: Highlights from SAP TechEd 2024 📢
entry.content:
The SAP TechEd 2024 event, themed “Fire of Innovations,” has once again set the stage for groundbreaking advancements in enterprise technology. Held virtually on October 8-9, this year’s conference showcased a plethora of new tools, updates, and strategies designed to empower businesses and developers alike. Here’s a deep dive into some of the most exciting announcements and innovations from the event.

1. Joule: The AI Copilot Revolution

One of the standout highlights was the unveiling of new capabilities for Joule, SAP’s generative AI copilot. Joule has been supercharged with collaborative AI agents that can perform complex, cross-disciplinary tasks. These agents are designed to break down silos within organizations, enhancing productivity and allowing human workers to focus on more creative and strategic activities.

For instance, in dispute management, autonomous AI agents can now analyze and resolve issues such as incorrect invoices or duplicate payments. In financial accounting, these agents streamline processes like bill payments and ledger updates, quickly addressing any inconsistencies.

2. SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) Enhancements

SAP continues to expand its Business Technology Platform (BTP), which is central to its cloud strategy. This year, SAP announced the expansion of BTP coverage through eight new SAP-managed data centers. Additionally, the SAP Discovery Center now offers a Quick-Account-Setup feature, enabling businesses to deploy specific use cases with just a click.

The integration of ABAP Cloud into SAP Build was another significant update. This integration allows developers to create and maintain ABAP Cloud projects directly from the SAP Build lobby, ensuring a seamless and efficient development process.

3. Innovations in SAP Build

SAP Build has been strengthened to support a “Clean Core” strategy, which is crucial for maintaining system consistency and upgradeability. The SAP Build Extensibility Wizard, introduced for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition, helps both key users and developers create on-stack and side-by-side extensions in an embedded experience.

4. Harnessing the Power of Data with SAP Knowledge Graph

SAP introduced the SAP Knowledge Graph, a next-generation solution that connects SAP data with rich business context. This tool is poised to help developers unlock the full value of SAP data, driving more informed decision-making and innovation.

5. Global Reach and Accessibility

This year’s SAP TechEd was a truly global event, with a free two-day virtual format that included keynotes, technical sessions, and developer enablement activities. The event was designed to be accessible to a worldwide audience, ensuring that everyone could benefit from the latest SAP innovations.

Conclusion

SAP TechEd 2024 has once again demonstrated SAP’s commitment to driving innovation and empowering businesses with cutting-edge technology. From AI advancements with Joule to enhancements in SAP BTP and SAP Build, the “Fire of Innovations” theme truly encapsulates the transformative potential of these new tools and strategies. As businesses continue to navigate the complexities of the digital age, SAP’s innovations provide a robust foundation for growth and success.

Stay tuned for more updates and insights from SAP TechEd as we continue to explore the future of enterprise technology.

Feel free to share your thoughts or ask any questions about the event! What innovation are you most excited about?

entry.title: Your guide to Guided Experiences powered by SAP Build - Frequently asked questions
entry.content:
Welcome everyone! 

In this blogpost, I will breakdown the most frequently asked questions about guided experiences, an innovative capability powered by SAP Build. I will address questions around how it works, the benefits it offers, common use cases and much more. Let’s get started on unlocking the full potential of guided experiences. 

🔐For those looking to dive deeper into the capability, I recommend exploring our expert blogpost, where you'll find a comprehensive breakdown of its features. 

***

✔️ What are guided experiences in SAP Build?

Guided experience is a capability within SAP Build, a business application development and automation solution with a comprehensive suite of low-code, pro-code, and generative AI tools. This capability enables business users to simplify complex business processes into self-service journeys that are easy to follow and execute. It allows users to create interactive, step-by-step guides for navigating and using applications.

✔️ What are the key aspects of guided experiences?

Key aspects of guided experiences are as below. 

  • Step-by-step guidance: Users are guided through a series of steps to complete a specific task or process performed within SAP Build Work Zone with use cases often spanning across multiple systems. 
  • Customizable content: Users can create custom content for the guided experiences, including text, images, and videos, to provide clear and concise instructions, with no- and low-code capabilities.
  • Interactive elements: Guided experiences can include elements like UI Cards and default widgets (image, text, content, etc.) to engage users and ensure smooth execution.

✔️ What are some common use cases for guided experiences?

Some use cases examples can be: 

  • Creating one simple employee onboarding journey for new employees
  • Creating step-based guide to deliver best practices, training and relevant material to empower sales teams
  • Simplified purchase order verification process while purchasing equipment
  • Creating a smooth onboarding journey for new suppliers
  • Seamless journey for processing invoices across channel partners
  • And many more...

✔️Which SAP Build solutions are included in guided experiences?

Guided experiences incorporate capabilities from all the low-code SAP Build solutions - SAP Build Work Zone, SAP Build Process Automation and SAP Build Apps.

✔️ How does guided experiences drive business value?

  • Enhanced User Experience: Empowers workforces with personalized digital experiences, boosting satisfaction, engagement, and retention.
  • Increased Productivity: Automates tasks and streamlines processes, enabling employees to focus on high-impact work.
  • Greater Agility: Rapidly deploys, modifies, and scale digital capabilities with intuitive tools, empowering business builders without IT dependency

✔️What is a 'wizard layout' in guided experiences?

Wizard layout the actual user interface in SAP Build Work Zone that the end-user work with. It allows users to create a step-by-step experience on any workpage. This layout can consist of one or multiple stages, each containing various steps. These steps or stages can be set as optional or mandatory, depending on the process requirements. Users can incorporate different UI elements available in Work Zone, such as tiles, widgets, UI cards, and other applications into each step.

✔️ What is a 'guided process' in guided experiences?

For more advanced processes, it is possible to create a ‘guided process’ as an artefact in SAP Build Process Automation and make use of the powerful capabilities of Process Automation. The guided process can then be deployed to SAP Build Work Zone and then used as the foundation/template when creating a wizard layout as an alternative option to creating the wizard manually directly in Work Zone.

✔️ What authorization is required for a user to create a Guided Experience in SAP Build Work Zone using a predefined template?

The user must have end-user permission/authorization. Any user with the permission to create and edit a work page can modify and create wizards within SAP Build Work Zone. When creating a guided experience wizard layout based on a ‘guided process’ template, the user requires access to a deployed template in either the public or another environment in Process Automation. To model and deploy the ‘guided process’ artefact, the advanced user permission is required in Process Automation side.

✔️ Can a user save their progress in SAP Build Work Zone for gradual competition?

Yes, progress of the user within the wizard layout is saved automatically so that they return and complete the steps another time – if needed.

✔️ Does the user mark completion of their own tasks or does Work Zone pick up on the status from other systems?

Work Zone has the ability to verify if a specific activity has been completed by the user. For mandatory steps, Work Zone ensures that the task is completed before allowing progress in the process. In each step, tasks / activities from other business systems could be incorporated via UI5 cards (where the task would then be completed). The UI card framework has also been enhanced to support interaction with the wizard layout to check the completion of a task. See here!

✔️ Are guided experiences available in SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition?

No, they are not available in SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition and there are currently no plans to enable this capability in standard edition either.

✔️ Are guided experiences available in SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone?

 Yes, additionally to SAP Build Work Zone, advanced edition, they are also available in SAP SuccessFactors Work Zone. 

✔️ Given the visibility scenarios in SAP Build Process Automation, what additional benefits does SAP Build Work Zone offer?

SAP Build Work Zone provides the consumption layer and easy wizard editor that can be conveniently redefined by content administrator at runtime. With SAP Build Process Automation ‘guided process’ as the process backbone for the wizard layout, the flexible design & content capabilities of Work Zone provide the user-facing capabilities. Additionally, aspects like UI5 cards enable flexible, context-rich integration of business data across SAP and 3rd party solutions in each individual wizard step.

✔️ Is it possible to for the end-user to restart the completed process/step?

Capabilities to restart a completed process are on the roadmap, which can then be enabled in the guided experience configuration. Currently a guided experience can enforce a restart when editing and publishing a new version; alternatively, it can be configured to allow existing processes to complete with the previous version until completion.

✔️ What guided experience capabilities are on the roadmap?

Looking ahead into the H2 2024 and beyond, many additional capabilities are planned for the SAP Build guided experience capability. For instance integration with process visibility, support for additional step UIs like forms, ability for users to start multiple guided experiences for the same template and more.

Further to this, additional business content examples – for example onboarding from SAP SuccessFactors – are being created to get started more easily. This business content can then be used as template and tweaked as required.

***

I hope this blogpost has addressed your questions. If you want to learn more, feel free to explore the additional resources listed below.

➤ SAP Build Work Zone on: sap.com / SAP Community

➤ SAP Build Process Automation on: sap.com / SAP Community 

➤ SAP Builders Community Group 

➤ Learning Journey: Implement and Administer SAP Build Work Zone | SAP Learning

➤ Learning Journey: Creating Processes and Automations with SAP Build Process Automation

➤ SAP TechEd session ft. guided experiences: (AD108) Enhance Business Efficiency with SAP Build 

➤ SAP Devtoberfest session: Guided Experiences with SAP Build

entry.title: GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, base: An Overview of the base edition bundle
entry.content:
GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, base: An Overview of the base edition

Since March 2023, customers who have selected SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition as the core ERP system to run their businesses have received additional entitlements beyond the core functionality. These added capabilities form one of two comprehensive bundles: GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, base and GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, premium.
 
In this blog, I will focus on the base edition bundle, providing a clear explanation of each entitlement and its business value. I will discuss the premium edition in a separate blog, which I will hyperlink here once created.

This blog is designed for business users and consultants with ERP experience, aiming to provide practical insights into each entitlement's purpose and when it’s most valuable. Please note, statements about adoption difficulty and required personas are based on my research and experience, and other cases may vary based on the specific business context etc.
Note 1: Hyperlinks to SAP4ME will take you directly to the relevant page if you're logged into a productive SAP4ME system and have suitable rights
Note 2: This blog speaks to the entitlements included as of 2024-10-17

Blog Outline:

  1. GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Base: Overview
  2. Core Entitlement: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  3. General Notes on Additional Entitlements
  4. Base Entitlement: Finance - Group Reporting
  5. Base Entitlement: BTP - SAP Build Process Automation
  6. Base Entitlement: BTP - SAP Build Workzone, Standard Edition
  7. Base Entitlement: BTP - CPEA Credits
  8. Base Entitlement: SAP Digital Access
  9. Base Entitlement: Ariba Buying, Base Edition
  10. Closing Remarks

 

  1. GROW with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Base: Overview
    The Base Edition comes in two SKUs: 8014771 for global markets and 8014782 for the Greater China region (which excludes some entitlements). This blog focuses on SKU 8014771.

    How to identify if you have this SKU:
    1. Log in to SAP for Me: Reporting > Purchased Solutions
    2. Under 'Orders by Solution Area,' select S/4HANA Public - your SKU will be listed on the right. If 8014771 appears with a valid date, you have the GROW Base Edition. If 8014761 appears, you have Premium. Other SKUs likely represent SKUs that do not include this full suite of entitlements.

  2. Core Entitlement: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
    The core entitlement includes:
    1. Services & Support: Access to support services like incident creation, onboarding advisor support, and the SAP Enterprise Support Portfolio, which offers learning content and services such as Continuous Quality Checks.
    2. Systems: Access to multiple systems (e.g., ERP production, test, and development systems) and related tenants. This also includes non-ERP systems like Cloud ALM, CBC, SAP for Me, and BTP systems.
    3. Functionality: Access to pre-configured scope items. Commonly used ones include J59 - Accounts Receivable and J60 - Accounts Payable. You can view and manage scope items through SAP Activate’s Availability and Dependencies of Scope Items accelerator.
    4. Full User Equivalent (FUE): Your contract includes a specific number of FUEs that govern how many business users can access core functionality. Check your licensed FUE quantity in SAP for Me: Reporting - License Consumption under metric CY334.
    5. Entitlement Measurement: Overuse is possible but monitored by SAP. Your account team will reach out in case of overconsumption.

  3. General Notes on Additional Entitlements
    What is an "entitlement"?
    An entitlement refers to the right to use a specific set of functionalities that may normally require extra payment or licenses. In the GROW Base Edition, certain features are included up to a threshold without extra software licensing costs. For example, Group Reporting is included up to 25 objects in the base edition.

    Are the "entitlements" completely free?
    The outlined entitlements mean there's no software licensing cost if you use up to the amount indicated. Usage beyond that quantity will require additional (paid) licenses, and you may need to consider some investment of business time and effort to adopt new features and functions, and in some cases you'll require consultants for the setup and implementation.

    What does "adoption" mean in this blog?
    I'm using 'adoption' to refer to the setup AND usage a software functionality. The setup or implementation is the process of adopting, and once it's setup and being actively used  - then I would then say the customer has adopted a feature/entitlement.
    Base Edition - Additional Entitlements:
    GROW with SAP Public Edition, base - additional entitlements
  4. Base Edition Entitlement: Group Reporting
    Business Case
    Group Reporting helps businesses that operate multiple subsidiaries or legal entities shorten the time needed for financial consolidation. It automates key activities and streamlines the data model, making it highly valuable for businesses with complex financial structures. For single-entity businesses, this functionality is not immediately relevant.

    Business Role Relevance
    Senior finance roles, such as Finance Managers and Group Controllers, will benefit from this functionality for managing financial consolidations.

    Entitlement
    The GROW Base Edition includes 25 Group Reporting objects. An object typically refers to a legal entity within a consolidation group (e.g., parent and subsidiary companies). Additional licenses are required for more than 25 objects. Official documentation is here

    License Consumption
    You can track your consumption using metric CH112 in SAP for Me under SAP4ME: Reporting -> License Consumption.

    Overusage
    It is possible to over-consume your allocated Group Reporting objects. SAP will periodically review your usage under metric CH112. You can license additional units by contacting SAP for SKU 8013710.

    Scope Item Relevance
    The main scope item for Group Reporting is 1SG. This measures whether data from group reporting has been posted into the system. You can validate its usage in Portfolio & Products - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Usage. Other Scope Items flagged as Group Reporting relevant are  287, 28B, 4R7, 2U6

    More Information
    For more detailed guidance on adopting Group Reporting, refer to this blog post. This deeper descriptions, webinar links, and crucially it outlines the key steps to adopting group reporting

    Key Adoption Notes:
    Expertise needed: You'll need a combination of BTP administrators and finance consultants for configuration and initial setup.
    Adoption Effort:  Rated 3 (on a scale from 1 to 5). You’ll need finance consultants, and technical consultants with expertise in BTP to set up this functionality.
    Not to be confused with: This entitlement does not include Group Reporting Data Collection, which must be licensed separately.

  5. Base Edition Entitlement: SAP Build Process Automation
    Business Case
    SAP Build Process Automation enables non-developers, such as key users or business process experts, to create bots or automated workflows based on a set of rules. This is valuable when no standard ERP functionality exists to meet a specific business need, for example, automating the creation of general ledger accounts.

    Business Role Relevance
    Consultants, process automation specialists, and business key users involved in implementing and running the ERP system will find this useful.

    Entitlement
    The base edition includes:
    20 Standard Users (CP278) – Can trigger or interact with a process.
    2 Advanced Users (CP279) – Can design, monitor, and administrate processes.
    1 Unattended Automation (CP283) – A bot that continuously monitors and executes a process.
    Documentation of these entitlements are here

    License Consumption
    You can track your consumption of CP278, CP279, and CP283 in SAP4ME - Reporting - License

    Overusage
    You are blocked from overconsuming these licenses. Once the entitlement limit is reached, additional licenses must be purchased or allocated through CPEA credits.

    Scope Item Relevance
    There is no specific scope item for SAP Build Process Automation.

    More Information
    This community page has overview videos and links to all other resources. This area of the API hub is where you find SAP pre-created templates.

    Key Adoption Notes
    Expertise Needed: You’ll need a BTP expert for the base setup. Then you'll need LoB experts e.g. finance experts to develop meaningful automations as per your needs. Setup steps are outlined here.
    Adoption Effort: 2/5 for platform setup by BTP experts; 2-4/5 for workflow creation depending on complexity.

  6. Base Edition Entitlement: SAP Build Workzone, Standard Edition

    Business Case
    SAP Build Workzone allows organizations to create a central homepage for employees with user-specific access to applications across various SAP systems. This enhances efficiency by providing direct links to necessary business apps for roles like sales managers or purchasing clerks. The more SAP systems used throughout your organization, the more value this solution will offer your organization.

    Business Role Relevance
    This functionality benefits employees across departments who frequently use SAP applications in their daily tasks.

    Entitlement
    You receive 1 Workzone user per FUE licensed. For example, if you have licensed 100 FUEs for the core, 100 users can access SAP Build Workzone.

    License Consumption
    You can track your consumption of CP264 in SAP4ME - Reporting - License Consumption

    Overusage
    Additional licenses or CPEA credits are required if the number of users exceeds your entitlement.

    Scope Item Relevance
    There is no scope item related to SAP Build Workzone usage.

    More Information
    More documentation is on the SAP Build Workzone Discovery Center.

    Key Adoption Notes
    Expertise Needed: A BTP global administrator for initial setup, followed by non-technical key users for managing pages.
    Adoption Effort: 2/5 for BTP experts; 3/5 for non-experts. View the setup steps in this tutorial.
    Not to be confused with: SAP Build Workzone is distinct from SAP Start, which a more lightweight solution without ability to consider other SAP system authorizations. SAP Build Workzone Advanced Edition is a different product without a migration path from the standard edition.

  7. Base Edition Entitlement: CPEA Credits

    Business Case
    CPEA Credits (Cloud Platform Enterprise Agreement) allow businesses to consume various BTP services within predefined limits without additional fees. These credits can be applied to services like Integration Suite, SAP Build Apps, and Cloud Identity Services. You can view the list of included services here in the Discovery Center. There's more nuance to the CPEA credit commercial model, which you can read about here.

    Business Role Relevance
    Technical teams managing BTP services will oversee consumption of this entitlement. The business areas that consume this depend on which services you're utilizing from BTP - many are in the technical / administration area.

    Entitlement
    Customers receive 1% of their net Annual Contract Value in CPEA credits, with a minimum value of €2,000 and a maximum value of €16,000.

    License Consumption
    You can view your CPEA credit balance in SAP4ME - Finance and Legal - Enterprise Agreements

    Scope Item Relevance
    CPEA credit usage is not tied to any scope item, as it applies across various BTP services.

    More Information
    Each service that can be paid for by CPEA credits are listed here in the Discovery Center - and this same resource provides additional information on each service.

    Key Adoption Notes
    Expertise Needed: BTP experts and business area owners are required to manage and utilize CPEA credits effectively.
    Adoption Effort: Varies depending on the BTP service being used.
    Not to be confused with: CPEA credits differ from BTPEA and EA credits, which operate similarly but cover different services.

  8. Base Edition Entitlement: SAP Digital Access

    Business Case
    SAP Digital Access governs how non-human interactions (such as external systems creating documents) are licensed. It is relevant when integrating third-party systems like Salesforce to trigger document creation within SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

    Business Role Relevance
    IT and integration specialists responsible for system integrations with non-SAP platforms will need to manage this entitlement.

    Entitlement
    The base edition includes 100,000 digital access documents per year.

    License Consumption
    Track your consumption by launching the License Compliance Digital Access app in the productive SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition system. This guidance document shows how to do this and provides other useful context on measuring your digital access usage for SAP S/4HANA

    Overusage
    It is possible to exceed your annual document allowance. SAP periodically reviews your consumption and the account team will reach out in the case of overconsumption.

    Scope Item Relevance
    This entitlement applies to external system interactions and is not tied to specific scope items.

    More Information
    Refer to the Digital Access documentation for a more detailed explanation.

    Key Adoption Notes
    Expertise Needed: No specific competency is needed. Entitlement consumption should be reviewed by the party responsible for your SAP system billing & commercials.
    Adoption Effort: 1. There's nothing to setup per se for Digital Access - you only be aware of it and monitor your consumption.
    Other Notes: SAP Digital Access is only necessary for non-SAP systems. If SAP systems trigger document creation, digital access is not consumed.

  9. Base Edition Entitlement: Ariba Buying, Base Edition

    Business Case
    Ariba Buying Base Edition allows employees to make internal purchase requisitions. It's scope is similar to the core ERP requisitioning scope of 18J, but Ariba Buying Base has an enhanced UX and has the capability to integrate with many advanced features, such as directly connecting to supplier catalogs and the Ariba Business network.

    Business Role Relevance
    All company employees can benefit from an improved user experience for purchase requisitions, particularly in organizations using self-service requisitioning.

    Entitlement
    There is no access limit for the base functionality, but additional licensing may be required for extended capabilities, such as linking to external supplier catalogs.

    License Consumption
    There are no limits to usage of this entitlement. It operates more like a standard scope item, but many of the functionalities that bring Ariba Buying Base to life will require additional licenses.

    Scope Item Relevance
    Scope item 4AI measures whether you've setup the communications arrangement required to use Ariba Buying base edition.

    More Information
    View an overview of Ariba Buying in this video demo - but note this video includes the utilization of catalogs that may require additional licensing.

    Key Adoption Notes
    Expertise Needed: BTP administrators and procurement experts are required for setup and catalog integration.
    Adoption Effort: Unclear
    Other Notes: A lot of the functionality’s value comes from adding catalogs and integrating with the broader Ariba Network, which may require additional licensing.

  10. Closing
    The intent of this blog was to give you a base understanding of the each entitlement in the base edition - what it is, and when it's most valuable.
    Any feedback regarding on the content including other use cases, other resources to link etc. are welcome and I'll periodically update this blog with improved information.

entry.title: Announcing the New "In-Metro" Disaster Recovery Solution for SAP BTP
entry.content:
Overview

We’re excited to unveil the latest enhancement to SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP):
The In-Metro Disaster Recovery (DR) solution.

This solution is designed to protect you against disasters affecting a single Availability Zone (AZ) with contractually committed SLAs RPO and RTO, offering enhanced resilience and uninterrupted operations for your cloud services.

 

What is "In-Metro" DR for SAP BTP?

The In-Metro DR solution is built to protect against local disasters by leveraging a Multi-AZ setup. It ensures that, in the event of a single AZ disaster, which is defined by SAP, services remain operational, data is protected, and business continues without significant downtime.

The key metrics are:

  • RPO (Recovery Point Objective): 5 minutes – Ensuring that data can be recovered with a maximum of 5 minutes' loss in case of a disaster.
  • RTO (Recovery Time Objective): 2 hours – Ensuring that the full system is restored and operational within 2 hours of an incident.

 

The Power of Multi-AZ Architecture

At the heart of In-Metro DR is the Multi-AZ (Multi Availability Zone) architecture, ensuring that your applications and databases are spread across different zones to safeguard against single-point failures.

This includes:

  • Active-active applications across 3 AZs: Applications are distributed across three AZs, with real-time data replication ensuring that if one AZ fails, the others automatically take over without impacting availability.
  • Active-passive databases across 2 AZs: Databases are set up in active-passive mode, with a failover mechanism in place to switch operations to a backup AZ if the primary one fails.

Through automatic failover, load balancing, and failure detection, the solution ensures that services stay online seamlessly. Internal stress tests, such as regular “Chaos Day” exercises, further reinforce the reliability of this DR solution.

 

Availability and Scope

 As of now, the In-Metro DR solution is not available for all SAP BTP services. While it already covers a wide range of services, its scope will continue to expand over time as we improve coverage and extend it to additional SAP BTP services. The services in scope will be regularly updated in the corresponding documents.

In-Metro DR is specifically designed for SAP-managed scenarios, meaning that customers using SAP BTP under SAP’s management can take advantage of the In-Metro DR setup without additional configuration or costs. However, for customer-managed scenarios, such as side-by-side extensions, the customer is responsible for configuring it accordingly (e.g. deploy the apps with multiple instances or configure data replicas on the persistency level).

 

 DR Planning and Regular Testing

 To ensure the highest levels of reliability:

  • The DR plan is updated every 12 months, ensuring that it incorporates the latest technology and best practices.
  • A DR test is conducted at least once a year to verify that the solution can meet its RPO and RTO commitments under real-world conditions.

The In-Metro DR solution currently supports SAP BTP regions hosted on both AWS and Azure cloud infrastructures, providing flexibility for your organization’s cloud setup.

 

 Next Steps

 By leveraging In-Metro DR within SAP-managed scenarios, you can trust that your critical data and services are protected with cutting-edge resilience, without the need for extra investment. For customer-managed scenarios, setting up corresponding HA and DR solutions will ensure your setup is equally resilient.

To explore which services are already covered and get all information, check the description in SAP Trust Center.

Alternatively, you can watch a deep dive into this new solution at SAP TechED: Watch the SAP TechED session on In-Metro DR.

entry.title: You ask, we answer - Ask Me Anything Session (Oct 24)
entry.content:
During SAP TechEd 2024, SAP unveiled significant improvements to SAP Build, which included making SAP S/4HANA extension development more efficient, introducing new generative AI features, and providing broader access to ABAP development tools. These changes empower the developer community to innovate more easily and with increased agility. 

You can read all about the announcements in Michael Ameling's blog post: Unveiling today: SAP Build meets ABAP Cloud! And the good news is: you can still access the session catalog to review the content and stay current with the latest innovations & enjoy Devtoberst sessions until October 25, check out the agenda here.

Now if you're curious about what you saw during the event and have some more questions, or you would like to dive deeper, here's a great opportunity:

We'll be hosting a live Ask Me Anything session on October 24, 3pm CET. You can watch it on YouTube, or click the preview above. In the session, the panel of experts will recap the features announced and answer your questions live. 

Can't join? No worries, drop your questions in the comments and the experts will answer them live for you to watch later 😉

Looking forward to seeing you there!

Cheers,
Maiara
